# Professional Development

## Personnel Management

TPL is committed to supporting employees in building the skills and careers they want. In practice this means:

- **Work plans** — Each staff member receives a semesterly work plan three times a year (Fall, Spring, and Summer). Work plans lay out professional development goals and responsibilities for the period and are co-created by the TPL leadership team and the person's manager.
- **1:1 meetings** — All staff and students have regular recurring 1:1 meetings with their supervisors for ongoing feedback about performance and professional development opportunities.
- **Project templates + structured review** — TPL project resources and structured review processes provide opportunities for staff and students to learn on the job.

TPL follows HKS processes and policies for professional development and performance management, including:

- Three and six-month performance check-ins for new employees
- Annual performance reviews
- School-wide pay and title equity evaluations
- A team feedback process to recognize each other and provide constructive feedback

Contract renewal is not guaranteed and is evaluated annually in the context of performance, funding, workload, and fit. The likelihood of renewal will be discussed during formal reviews.

## 1:1 Meetings

Staff and students all have regular recurring 1:1 meetings with their supervisors. Performance should be discussed in every meeting. Come prepared to:

- Provide updates on your work
- Raise any blockers or concerns
- Discuss your professional development goals
- Update your shared agenda document ahead of time

## Work Plans

Each staff member receives a semesterly work plan three times a year. Work plans:

- Lay out professional development goals and responsibilities for the period
- Are co-created by the TPL leadership team and the person's manager
- Are discussed and iterated upon between the staff member and their manager
- Major changes require approval from the leadership team

During work plan meetings, managers and their reports should also reflect on the prior semester's work plan to discuss what went well and what can be improved.

## Performance Reviews

### Three-Month Orientation Review
Harvard HR requires managers to conduct a 3-month review with new hires and document it in PeopleSoft. TPL sees this as an opportunity to:

- Provide comprehensive feedback about how things are going
- Set clear goals for the remainder of the first year
- Convey that expectations ramp up at 3 months

### Annual Review
Annual reviews align with the end of the fiscal year and take place in June. TPL sees this as an opportunity to:

- Provide comprehensive feedback about the past year
- Set clear goals for the coming year
- Discuss performance issues and career growth

Note that title and compensation are handled separately at HKS on a different timeframe.

## Requesting Letters of Recommendation

1. **Send the request to your manager** who can help coordinate the most appropriate letter writer. If your manager is no longer with TPL, contact Caroline who can coordinate with Elizabeth.
2. **Give as much notice as possible** — ideally at least one month
3. **Include the following information:**
   - A short description of the program or position you're applying for
   - Your goals and how the opportunity will help you get there
   - Submission process details (deadline, how the system works)
   - Anything you want highlighted in the letter (skills, experience, examples of work)
   - Who else is writing letters for the same position
   - A few sentences of specific details from your experience at TPL
4. **Send a follow-up reminder** about one week before the deadline
